> walk me through how the rate limiter handles burst traffic

The limiter uses a token bucket with a refill rate of 100/s and a burst
capacity of 500. Let me trace a burst scenario through the code so the
behaviour is concrete.

  Read(src/limiter/bucket.go)
  ⎿  Read 156 lines

  Read(src/limiter/middleware.go)
  ⎿  Read 92 lines

When a burst of 600 requests arrives at t=0:
  - first 500 consume the bucket immediately (HTTP 200)
  - request 501 sees an empty bucket, computes wait = (1 - tokens) / 100 = 10ms
  - subsequent requests serialize behind that wait, draining at 100/s

So the worst-case latency under burst is bounded by 1s (the time to refill
the entire bucket once exhausted). That matches the SLO target.

Want me to add a test that exercises this exact scenario?

[Approaching 5-hour limit]
